question_text: Which of the following best explains why foods like nuts and oils contain more calories than fruits and vegetables?

options.label: A
options.text: They taste better and have stronger flavours

options.label: B
options.text: They contain more fat and protein, which store more chemical energy per gram than carbohydrates

options.label: C
options.text: They have been stored for longer, so more energy has accumulated

options.label: D
options.text: They are more expensive, so manufacturers add extra calories to them

explanation: Fats and proteins contain more chemical energy per gram than carbohydrates because of their molecular structure; fats store about 9 calories per gram while carbohydrates store about 4 calories per gram. Nuts and oils are high in fat, making them energy-dense foods, whereas fruits and vegetables contain mainly water and carbohydrates, so they have fewer calories by weight.
